title: Persistent IP Registry with Auth-Gated Submission and Public Search

requirements.text: Persist all IP registration form fields to on-chain stable storage in the backend. The backend actor must store submitted IP records in a stable data structure (e.g., stable var array or HashMap) so that records survive canister upgrades and accumulate over time as a growing database.

quotes: all fields should be saved from a registered IP into our database. This should be committed to memory and searchable.
every submission is committed to memory and searchable by everyone

acceptanceCriteria: All fields from the Register IP form are saved when a user submits a registration.
Saved records persist across canister upgrades (stable storage).
Records submitted by different users all appear in the database.
The number of stored records grows with each new submission.

requirements.text: Require Internet Identity authentication before a user can submit a new IP registration. On the Register IP page, if the user is not authenticated, show a prompt/button to sign in via Internet Identity. The registration form and submission action must be blocked until the user is logged in.

quotes: for people I share the link with they should still need to sign in or create an account like before to create an IP
users must sign in or create an account via Internet Identity before registering a new IP

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users visiting the Register IP page see a sign-in prompt instead of the registration form.
After signing in via Internet Identity, the registration form becomes accessible.
Submitting an IP record without an active Internet Identity session is rejected.

requirements.text: Allow anyone with the link to browse and search all submitted IP records on the IP Database page without requiring login. The backend must expose a public query endpoint for listing and searching all records, and the frontend must not gate the IP Database page behind authentication.

quotes: they should be viewable by everyone
Viewing records: Everyone can see and search the IP database (no login needed)

acceptanceCriteria: The IP Database page loads and displays all submitted records for unauthenticated visitors.
Search and filter functionality works without login.
Records submitted by any user are visible to all visitors.
No authentication prompt is shown on the IP Database page.

requirements.text: Expose backend query functions to support full-text or field-based search across all persisted IP records (by title, category, jurisdiction, description, etc.) so the existing search and filter UI on the IP Database page operates against real on-chain data.

quotes: This should be committed to memory and searchable.

acceptanceCriteria: A public query function returns all IP records matching a search term or filter criteria.
The IP Database frontend search and filter controls return live results from the backend.
Searching by title, category, and jurisdiction returns the correct matching records.

constraints: All data must be stored on-chain in the Internet Computer canister using Motoko stable storage — no external databases.
Authentication must use Internet Identity only.
Public browsing and search must not require any login.
Do not edit any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Admin moderation or record deletion is not in scope.
Email notifications or off-chain data storage are not in scope.
Paid submission or token-gating is not in scope.
Editing or updating previously submitted IP records is not in scope.
